foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Fluid resuscitation is the cornerstone of shock management; understanding fluid compartments and electrolyte shifts is essential for rational volume replacement

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Shock represents a failure of homeostatic mechanisms; understanding normal compensatory responses (baroreceptor reflexes, RAAS) is needed to comprehend shock pathophysiology

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Hemorrhagic shock requires blood product resuscitation; massive transfusion protocols are critical in managing class III/IV hemorrhagic shock

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Major burns cause distributive and hypovolemic shock through massive fluid shifts and capillary leak requiring Parkland formula-guided resuscitation

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Intraoperative and postoperative shock recognition and management is a core perioperative competency

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Septic shock is a major type of distributive shock encountered in surgical practice requiring source control and antimicrobial therapy

objectives: SU2.1: Describe Pathophysiology of shock, types of shock & principles of
SU2.2: Describe the clinical features of shock and its appropriate treatment.
SU2.3: Communicate and counsel patients and families about the A/C
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Shock across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
